text: 'Across all three pilots' is false. v2 decisions stratum is 10/12 pooled and Sonnet 4/4 on actual decision targets; only v3/v3b prose-weighted subset fails 0/6. Draft over-generalized from the wrong experiments.
Proposed decisions/* expansion issue (issue B) does not cover its own benchmark: T29 is projects/drone-lab, not decisions/*. A decisions-only fix cannot satisfy the 6-target subset.

Token-overlap method used 'content + namespace + key + title' but FTS indexes content+namespace+key+tags — title is not indexed, tags was omitted. Method also didn't model strict-AND vs relaxed-OR fallback or live tokenizer behavior.

Three failure modes, not one: (a) meta-intent zero overlap (T9/T10/T35/T47 in v3b), (b) T49 entry_type formulator bug in v3 and v3b, (c) T47 namespace='meta/' formulator bug in v3 (fixed in v3b). Each implies a different intervention.

Consolidation worker architecture cost understated: it currently scopes to projects/* and clients/* log entries and writes synthesis per namespace. Extending to decisions/* is not a light split — it broadens worker namespace floor, adds LLM/cadence coupling, and creates uneven retrieval surfaces under partial failure.

'Close #38 + file two new issues' is weaker than 'edit #38 in place.' Closing loses the audit trail of why the original framing was wrong, duplicates a live munin-zero workstream, and spends coordination budget during an active cross-repo deliverable.

Acceptance-criterion rewrite is a goalpost move if presented as replacement. Version it as a new benchmark (topical-decisions) alongside v3/v3b, not instead of. Replacing the failing test with an easier test and declaring victory is not resolution.

'Do NOT implement bm25 weights + title boosts' is too categorical. T29/T49 have partial indexed overlap so ranking could matter on a subset. Relaxed-OR fallback means weighting can matter after fallback. Title boost belongs in a separate schema/indexing discussion because title isn't indexed.
Draft missed that projects/munin-zero already has a cross-repo query-formulation experiment active in its next steps. Filing a new munin-zero issue duplicates live workstream.

Revised #38 title ('fails under v3/v3b meta-intents') still collapses the three-failure-mode taxonomy Claude just conceded. T49 is an entry_type formulator bug, not a meta-intent miss — collapsing them in the title routes the query-formulator bug back into obscurity.

'Tag v3/v3b as expected failure under lexical' is premature. The benchmark is still mixed (4 hard-lexical + 1 entry_type bug + 1 historical namespace bug). Tag only after strata are split by cause; otherwise the label is cosmetic dashboard hygiene.

Action plan step 3 ('comment on munin-zero status') is still file-and-forget. No artifact, no owner, no due date, no 'done' definition. In a week with an at-risk gate memo commitment, a comment-only handoff is exactly how benchmark versioning gets displaced. Must produce the artifact or an explicitly owned issue.

'Defer bm25/title/expansion' has no re-entry/stop condition. Is the trigger: new benchmark file exists, new benchmark rerun completes, or T29/T49-class cases still miss after rerun? Without that, defer stays open-ended and leaves 'done' underspecified.
